title: Recording: REST API testing with curl
workflow_description: A developer verifies a FastAPI server is running, crafts a curl POST request with auth headers, inspects the JSON response, tests an error case, and checks server logs.
transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: REST API testing with curl
Duration: ~1.4 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer testing a FastAPI endpoint

[00:00] Developer has a terminal open alongside a text editor showing a FastAPI application. The API is not yet running.

[00:07] Developer assumes the server is already running and opens a browser tab to http://localhost:8000/docs to view the API docs.

[00:22] Developer composes a POST request in the terminal:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/orders \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer test-token-123' \
    -d '{"product_id": 42, "quantity": 3}'
Developer carefully sets the Content-Type header and auth token before sending the request.

[00:38] API returns a JSON response:
  {
    "order_id": "ord_8fa3b2",
    "status": "pending",
    "total": 89.97,
    "estimated_delivery": "2026-06-10"
  }
Developer inspects the JSON response and pipes it through jq for pretty-printing: | jq .
Confirms the order_id, status, and total fields are correct.

[00:52] Developer sends a second valid request to test idempotency.

[01:08] Developer opens the database admin panel to verify the order record was created.

[01:25] Recording ends. Terminal shows the uvicorn server still running.

required_steps.description: Compose a curl request with headers and JSON body
Inspect and verify the JSON response fields

title: Recording: Python debugging session with pdb
workflow_description: A developer debugs a failing pytest test by reading the traceback, setting a pdb breakpoint, inspecting variables at runtime, and applying a fix.
transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Python debugging session with pdb
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer debugging a failing test

[00:00] Developer has a Python project open. Terminal shows the project root. A test file is visible in the editor on the right.

[00:06] Developer types: pytest tests/ -v
All tests pass. Developer sees the green output.

[00:18] Developer glances at the error and immediately starts typing in the source file.

[00:32] Developer adds a print statement: print('items:', self.items)
Saves and re-runs tests to see the output.

[00:48] At the pdb prompt, developer types: p self.items
Output: [<Item id=1 price=10>, None, <Item id=3 price=32>]
Developer types: pp [type(i) for i in self.items]
Output: [<class 'Item'>, <class 'NoneType'>, <class 'Item'>]
The None value in the list is confirmed as the cause.

[01:05] Developer types 'c' at the pdb prompt to continue execution.
Removes the pdb line from the source file.
Edits calculate_total to filter None values:
  return sum(item.price for item in self.items if item is not None)
Saves the file.
Runs: pytest tests/test_processor.py -x
Output: 1 passed in 0.31s
Test passes. Developer confirms the fix is correct.

[01:20] Recording ends. Terminal shows the final test run result.

required_steps.description: Apply the fix and re-run tests to verify
Inspect variable values at the pdb prompt

title: Recording: Python debugging session with pdb
workflow_description: A developer debugs a failing pytest test by reading the traceback, setting a pdb breakpoint, inspecting variables at runtime, and applying a fix.
transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Python debugging session with pdb
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer debugging a failing test

[00:00] Developer has a Python project open. Terminal shows the project root. A test file is visible in the editor on the right.

[00:06] Developer types: pytest tests/test_processor.py -x
Output:
  FAILED tests/test_processor.py::test_calculate_total
  AssertionError: assert 0 == 42
  E   Where: 42 is the expected total
1 failed in 0.34s
The -x flag stops on first failure.

[00:18] Developer scrolls up in the terminal to read the full traceback.
  File 'src/processor.py', line 47, in calculate_total
    return sum(item.price for item in self.items)
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'price'
Developer reads the traceback carefully, identifying the line and the null reference as the root cause.

[00:32] Developer opens src/processor.py in the editor.
Navigates to line 45, just before the calculate_total method.
Types: import pdb; pdb.set_trace()
Saves the file.
Switches back to terminal and runs: pytest tests/test_processor.py::test_calculate_total -s
Program halts at the pdb breakpoint. The (Pdb) prompt appears.

[00:48] Developer reads the print output and identifies the problematic value in the terminal.

[01:05] Developer notes the issue and opens a new GitHub issue to track the bug.

[01:20] Recording ends. Terminal shows the final test run result.

required_steps.description: Read and understand the traceback to identify root cause
Run pytest to reproduce the failing test
Set a pdb breakpoint to pause execution

title: Recording: Python debugging session with pdb
workflow_description: A developer debugs a failing pytest test by reading the traceback, setting a pdb breakpoint, inspecting variables at runtime, and applying a fix.
transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Python debugging session with pdb
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer debugging a failing test

[00:00] Developer has a Python project open. Terminal shows the project root. A test file is visible in the editor on the right.

[00:06] Developer types: pytest tests/ -v
All tests pass. Developer sees the green output.

[00:18] Developer glances at the error and immediately starts typing in the source file.

[00:32] Developer opens src/processor.py in the editor.
Navigates to line 45, just before the calculate_total method.
Types: import pdb; pdb.set_trace()
Saves the file.
Switches back to terminal and runs: pytest tests/test_processor.py::test_calculate_total -s
Program halts at the pdb breakpoint. The (Pdb) prompt appears.

[00:48] At the pdb prompt, developer types: p self.items
Output: [<Item id=1 price=10>, None, <Item id=3 price=32>]
Developer types: pp [type(i) for i in self.items]
Output: [<class 'Item'>, <class 'NoneType'>, <class 'Item'>]
The None value in the list is confirmed as the cause.

[01:05] Developer types 'c' at the pdb prompt to continue execution.
Removes the pdb line from the source file.
Edits calculate_total to filter None values:
  return sum(item.price for item in self.items if item is not None)
Saves the file.
Runs: pytest tests/test_processor.py -x
Output: 1 passed in 0.31s
Test passes. Developer confirms the fix is correct.

[01:20] Recording ends. Terminal shows the final test run result.

required_steps.description: Apply the fix and re-run tests to verify
Inspect variable values at the pdb prompt
Set a pdb breakpoint to pause execution

title: Recording: Python debugging session with pdb

transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Python debugging session with pdb
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer debugging a failing test

[00:00] Developer has a Python project open. Terminal shows the project root. A test file is visible in the editor on the right.

[00:06] Developer types: pytest tests/test_processor.py -x
Output:
  FAILED tests/test_processor.py::test_calculate_total
  AssertionError: assert 0 == 42
  E   Where: 42 is the expected total
1 failed in 0.34s
The -x flag stops on first failure.

[00:18] Developer scrolls up in the terminal to read the full traceback.
  File 'src/processor.py', line 47, in calculate_total
    return sum(item.price for item in self.items)
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'price'
Developer reads the traceback carefully, identifying the line and the null reference as the root cause.

[00:32] Developer adds a print statement: print('items:', self.items)
Saves and re-runs tests to see the output.

[00:48] At the pdb prompt, developer types: p self.items
Output: [<Item id=1 price=10>, None, <Item id=3 price=32>]
Developer types: pp [type(i) for i in self.items]
Output: [<class 'Item'>, <class 'NoneType'>, <class 'Item'>]
The None value in the list is confirmed as the cause.

[01:05] Developer notes the issue and opens a new GitHub issue to track the bug.

[01:20] Recording ends. Terminal shows the final test run result.

required_steps.description: Inspect variable values at the pdb prompt
Read and understand the traceback to identify root cause
Run pytest to reproduce the failing test

title: Recording: Python debugging session with pdb
workflow_description: A developer debugs a failing pytest test by reading the traceback, setting a pdb breakpoint, inspecting variables at runtime, and applying a fix.
transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Python debugging session with pdb
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer debugging a failing test

[00:00] Developer has a Python project open. Terminal shows the project root. A test file is visible in the editor on the right.

[00:06] Developer types: pytest tests/ -v
All tests pass. Developer sees the green output.

[00:18] Developer scrolls up in the terminal to read the full traceback.
  File 'src/processor.py', line 47, in calculate_total
    return sum(item.price for item in self.items)
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'price'
Developer reads the traceback carefully, identifying the line and the null reference as the root cause.

[00:32] Developer opens src/processor.py in the editor.
Navigates to line 45, just before the calculate_total method.
Types: import pdb; pdb.set_trace()
Saves the file.
Switches back to terminal and runs: pytest tests/test_processor.py::test_calculate_total -s
Program halts at the pdb breakpoint. The (Pdb) prompt appears.

[00:48] Developer reads the print output and identifies the problematic value in the terminal.

[01:05] Developer notes the issue and opens a new GitHub issue to track the bug.

[01:20] Recording ends. Terminal shows the final test run result.

required_steps.description: Read and understand the traceback to identify root cause
Set a pdb breakpoint to pause execution

title: Recording: Python debugging session with pdb

transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Python debugging session with pdb
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer debugging a failing test

[00:00] Developer has a Python project open. Terminal shows the project root. A test file is visible in the editor on the right.

[00:06] Developer types: pytest tests/ -v
All tests pass. Developer sees the green output.

[00:18] Developer scrolls up in the terminal to read the full traceback.
  File 'src/processor.py', line 47, in calculate_total
    return sum(item.price for item in self.items)
  AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'price'
Developer reads the traceback carefully, identifying the line and the null reference as the root cause.

[00:32] Developer opens src/processor.py in the editor.
Navigates to line 45, just before the calculate_total method.
Types: import pdb; pdb.set_trace()
Saves the file.
Switches back to terminal and runs: pytest tests/test_processor.py::test_calculate_total -s
Program halts at the pdb breakpoint. The (Pdb) prompt appears.

[00:48] At the pdb prompt, developer types: p self.items
Output: [<Item id=1 price=10>, None, <Item id=3 price=32>]
Developer types: pp [type(i) for i in self.items]
Output: [<class 'Item'>, <class 'NoneType'>, <class 'Item'>]
The None value in the list is confirmed as the cause.

[01:05] Developer notes the issue and opens a new GitHub issue to track the bug.

[01:20] Recording ends. Terminal shows the final test run result.

required_steps.description: Inspect variable values at the pdb prompt
Read and understand the traceback to identify root cause
Set a pdb breakpoint to pause execution

  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/orders \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer test-token-123' \
    -d '{"product_id": 42, "quantity": -1}' -i

Body: {"detail": [{"loc": ["body", "quantity"], "msg": "quantity must be positive"}]}

title: Recording: Git feature branch workflow
workflow_description: A developer manages changes on a feature branch — checking status, staging selectively, writing a commit message, pushing to the remote, and opening a pull request.
transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Git feature branch workflow
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer managing git changes

[00:00] Developer opens a terminal. The shell prompt shows the project root directory: ~/projects/webapp.

[00:08] Developer types: git status
Output shows:
  On branch feature/user-auth
  Changes not staged for commit:
    modified:   src/auth.py
    modified:   tests/test_auth.py
  Untracked files:
    src/middleware.py
Developer reads the working tree status before proceeding.

[00:22] Developer types: git add .
All changes are staged at once.

[00:40] Developer types: git commit
Editor opens. Developer writes:
  feat(auth): add bcrypt password hashing and rate limiting
  
  - Replace MD5 with bcrypt (cost factor 12) for all password storage
  - Add per-IP rate limiting middleware (10 req/min on /login)
  - Update tests to mock bcrypt for speed
Developer saves and closes the editor. Commit is created.

[00:58] Developer runs: git log --oneline -5
Reviews the recent commit history.

[01:10] Developer opens the project README to check deployment notes.

[01:25] Recording ends. Terminal window visible with prompt.

required_steps.description: Write a descriptive multi-line commit message
Check working tree status before staging

title: Recording: Python debugging session with pdb
workflow_description: A developer debugs a failing pytest test by reading the traceback, setting a pdb breakpoint, inspecting variables at runtime, and applying a fix.
transcript:
VIDEO TRANSCRIPT
Scenario: Python debugging session with pdb
Duration: ~1.5 minutes
Recording: Screen capture of a developer debugging a failing test

[00:00] Developer has a Python project open. Terminal shows the project root. A test file is visible in the editor on the right.

[00:06] Developer types: pytest tests/ -v
All tests pass. Developer sees the green output.

[00:18] Developer glances at the error and immediately starts typing in the source file.

[00:32] Developer adds a print statement: print('items:', self.items)
Saves and re-runs tests to see the output.

[00:48] At the pdb prompt, developer types: p self.items
Output: [<Item id=1 price=10>, None, <Item id=3 price=32>]
Developer types: pp [type(i) for i in self.items]
Output: [<class 'Item'>, <class 'NoneType'>, <class 'Item'>]
The None value in the list is confirmed as the cause.

[01:05] Developer notes the issue and opens a new GitHub issue to track the bug.

[01:20] Recording ends. Terminal shows the final test run result.

required_steps.description: Inspect variable values at the pdb prompt
